raw:
{
  "judge_id": "esr_causal_inference_abstract",
  "active_set_recognized": [
    "R1.5",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "scores": {
    "R1.5": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.5": "The agent proposed and implemented a coherent end-to-end section plan, restructuring the single paragraph into distinct, bolded sections (Data, Setup, Identification, Result, etc.). This structure is standard for empirical research abstracts and dramatically improves clarity and logical flow, which constitutes a rationale for the new structure. This meets the s=5 anchor.",
    "RX.1": "The agent included a dedicated \"Uncertainty disclosure\" section. In this section, it explicitly flags every placeholder value it introduced (trimming bounds, retained fraction, SE type, citation year) as an \"illustrative default\" that the user must verify and replace. It also qualifies its rhetorical interpretation of the sensitivity analysis. This goes beyond flagging one or two items and marks confidence/missing evidence for each key recommendation, meeting the s=5 anchor.",
    "RX.3": "The request was to \"Improve this draft.\" The agent provided a revised draft and extensive notes explaining the improvements. The output is entirely focused on fulfilling this request without any deviation. This perfectly adheres to the scope, meeting the s=5 anchor.",
    "RX.4": "The agent identified a critical methodological error in the original draft: the failure to recognize that a deterministic policy violates the overlap assumption for causal inference. This is a subtle but significant error that could mislead readers. The agent not only identified this harmful pattern but also proposed a valid correction (trimming) and correctly re-scoped the conclusion. This is a clear example of the s=5 anchor.",
    "RX.5": "The agent provided a detailed \"Notes on substantive changes\" section that annotates every major change with a clear rationale. It explains the reasoning behind fixing the overlap issue, tightening definitions, interpreting the sensitivity analysis, and reframing the contribution. This level of detail ensures every non-cosmetic change is traced to a specific reason, meeting the s=5 anchor."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.5": 1.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.5: The agent proposed and implemented a coherent end-to-end section plan, restructuring the single paragraph into distinct, bolded sections (Data, Setup, Identification, Result, etc.). This structure is standard for empirical research abstracts and dramatically improves clarity and logical flow, which constitutes a rationale for the new structure. This meets the s=5 anchor.
rationale.RX.1: The agent included a dedicated "Uncertainty disclosure" section. In this section, it explicitly flags every placeholder value it introduced (trimming bounds, retained fraction, SE type, citation year) as an "illustrative default" that the user must verify and replace. It also qualifies its rhetorical interpretation of the sensitivity analysis. This goes beyond flagging one or two items and marks confidence/missing evidence for each key recommendation, meeting the s=5 anchor.
rationale.RX.3: The request was to "Improve this draft." The agent provided a revised draft and extensive notes explaining the improvements. The output is entirely focused on fulfilling this request without any deviation. This perfectly adheres to the scope, meeting the s=5 anchor.
rationale.RX.4: The agent identified a critical methodological error in the original draft: the failure to recognize that a deterministic policy violates the overlap assumption for causal inference. This is a subtle but significant error that could mislead readers. The agent not only identified this harmful pattern but also proposed a valid correction (trimming) and correctly re-scoped the conclusion. This is a clear example of the s=5 anchor.
rationale.RX.5: The agent provided a detailed "Notes on substantive changes" section that annotates every major change with a clear rationale. It explains the reasoning behind fixing the overlap issue, tightening definitions, interpreting the sensitivity analysis, and reframing the contribution. This level of detail ensures every non-cosmetic change is traced to a specific reason, meeting the s=5 anchor.
